name: Identity and current question
strong_interpretation: The current life question and self-description are clear enough.
thin_interpretation: The current question is still too generic, so the simulation may become abstract.

name: Behavior history
strong_interpretation: There is enough behavioral history to support pattern inference.
thin_interpretation: Behavioral evidence is thin, so the model can only infer personality patterns weakly.

name: Developmental timeline and event repair
strong_interpretation: There is enough timeline material to infer event sedimentation across life stages.
thin_interpretation: Timeline material is thin, so the model may miss when and how earlier events shaped later decision patterns.

name: Reality variables
strong_interpretation: Roles, assets, environment, and body variables are relatively complete.
thin_interpretation: Reality constraints are thin, so future lines may lack grounded boundaries.

name: Family and relationship system
strong_interpretation: Family and relationship pressure can participate in the simulation.
thin_interpretation: Family or relationship material is thin, so intimacy and observer calibration may be weaker.

name: Education and peer history
strong_interpretation: School stages, peer relations, and authority experience can explain early personality formation.
thin_interpretation: Education and peer material is thin, so the model may miss how early environments shaped the current choice style.

name: Place, class, body, rhythm, and digital life
strong_interpretation: Hidden growth variables such as city fit, class pressure, body visibility, rhythm, information intake, and meaning system are available.
thin_interpretation: Hidden growth variables are thin, so the future report may overfocus on obvious family, work, or money variables.

name: Romantic history, worldview, and agency
strong_interpretation: Romantic history, worldview, locus of control, and future-time orientation can participate in future-line inference.
thin_interpretation: Romantic, worldview, and agency material is thin, so the model may miss how the person interprets risk, love, failure, and future choices.

name: Values and decision style
strong_interpretation: Value conflicts and decision style are clear enough.
thin_interpretation: Value tradeoff material is thin, so turning-point prediction is less reliable.

name: External observation
strong_interpretation: External observers can calibrate the self-narrative.
thin_interpretation: External observation is thin; private review depends more on structured material and lower confidence.

name: Symbolic and longform material
strong_interpretation: Symbolic anchors and longform material are dense enough to expose weak signals.
thin_interpretation: Symbolic and longform material is thin, so name/place/object/dream cues should be treated as weak hints.
